dataset_title: Individualized fMRI-guided rTMS combined with Self-System Therapy versus mindfulness exercises in treatment-resistant depression
summary_en: This OpenNeuro dataset (ds008064.v1.0.0) was only published/registered on 2026-06-29, i.e. about three weeks before this search (2026-07-21). Resolving the DOI in OpenAlex returns work id W7166566292 with cited_by_count = 0 and referenced_works_count = 0 -- no works currently cite this dataset record. A direct OpenAlex citation-graph query (filter=cites:W7166566292) could not be completed live due to an OpenAlex API rate/budget limit encountered during this session, but the work's own cited_by_count field (0) is consistent with zero citing works for a record this new. As a fallback, Europe PMC was searched for the dataset identifiers '"ds008064"' and '"openneuro.ds008064"' -- both returned 0 hits, confirming no indexed literature currently references this specific dataset accession. A broader Europe PMC search on the trial concept ('"Self-System Therapy" AND rTMS') returns 11 results, but all of these are precursor/background papers describing earlier phases of the same research program (e.g., Neacsiu et al. 2018 J ECT on combining Self-System Therapy with fMRI-guided TMS; Luber et al. 2017 NeuroImage on imaging-guided TMS individualization; Davis et al. 2023 Int J Clin Health Psychol network analysis of the combined rTMS+psychotherapy treatment; Neacsiu et al. 2026 Behav Brain Res) -- none of these post-date or cite the ds008064 dataset release, since they predate its 2026-06-29 publication. Conclusion: as of 2026-07-21, no published work has yet used this specific OpenNeuro dataset. The dataset represents genuine white space -- the citation graph is empty and the only related literature is the study team's own prior/companion papers on the underlying clinical trial paradigm, not secondary analyses of this shared neuroimaging data.
notes: OpenAlex live citation query (filter=cites:<id>) failed with HTTP 429 'Insufficient budget / rate limit exceeded' (dailyRemainingUsd: 0, retryAfter ~16500s) during this session; conclusion of 0 citing works is corroborated independently by the resolved work's own cited_by_count=0 field and by a negative Europe PMC full-text/abstract search on the dataset accession string, so is considered reliable despite the failed direct citation-list call.
